title: ATDD always active by default for build workflows
story: As a developer using /isdlc build, I want ATDD protocol to run without needing a --atdd flag so that every acceptance criterion has a corresponding test without me having to remember an opt-in switch.
acceptance_criteria: Build workflow runs Phase 05 ATDD scaffold generation without any flag
Legacy --atdd flag has no effect (and triggers no special handling)

title: ATDD bridge default-active for existing-project discovery
story: As a developer onboarding an existing codebase via /discover, I want the ATDD bridge sub-phase to run by default so that my reverse-engineered ACs are ready for downstream ATDD consumption without a separate flag.
acceptance_criteria: Sub-phase 1d is skippable via atdd.enabled: false in config

title: Config surface for ATDD behavior
story: As a framework user, I want to configure ATDD behavior via .isdlc/config.json so that I can relax specific knobs (e.g., GWT strictness) for edge cases without disabling ATDD entirely.

title: Consistent config-driven ATDD across all touchpoints
story: As a maintainer, I want ATDD behavior driven from one config surface (not scattered conditionals) so that ATDD semantics cannot drift between hooks, phase agents, and workflow configs.
acceptance_criteria: Zero when: atdd_mode guards in iteration-requirements.json
Phase 05/06 delegations receive atdd.* via GATE REQUIREMENTS INJECTION

title: GWT enforcement via config
story: As a test-strategy author, I want Phase 05 to hard-block on non-GWT ACs when require_gwt: true so that the knob name matches its behavior and the contract is clear.
acceptance_criteria: require_gwt=false + non-GWT AC generates best-effort scaffold

title: RED to GREEN tracking via config
story: As a developer working through ATDD scaffolds, I want test-watcher to record RED to GREEN transitions when track_red_green: true so that my progress is visible in atdd-checklist.json.

title: Priority-ordered test completion via config
story: As a developer enforcing P0 first discipline, I want checkpoint-router to require tests to complete in priority order when enforce_priority_order: true so that critical behaviors ship first.

title: Master ATDD kill switch
story: As a framework user with a legitimate need to opt out of ATDD entirely, I want atdd.enabled: false to disable all ATDD behaviors so that I have a single escape hatch without flipping each sub-knob individually.

title: Documentation reflects config-driven model
story: As a new contributor reading the framework docs, I want CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, HOOKS.md, and AGENTS.md to describe config-driven ATDD so that I understand the current surface without chasing stale flag references.
